summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates a covered call wheel strategy on HIMS and Nphase stocks, selling weekly calls at predetermined strike prices to generate consistent premium income. He earned $68 this week ($24 from HIMS, $44 from Nphase) on a $13,018 deployed capital base, emphasizing the trade-off between guaranteed premium and potential upside capture.
takeaways: Sell covered calls at strike prices you're willing to sell shares at (HIMS: bought at $50, sold calls at $50 strike)
Accept partial gains rather than maximum gains; 'your goal is not to make all of the money, just some of it'
Know your break-even: calculate both premium gain AND stock gain/loss if assigned (e.g., Nphase position: $144 premium + $20.34 stock gain = $164.34 total gain)
If shares fall below purchase price, selling higher strike calls risks assignment at a loss (HIMS now at $47 vs. bought at $50: $49 strike could lock in a loss)
Target weekly premium of $180+ on a $50k portfolio; adjust strike selection based on current price and desired assignment probability
Roll calls weekly if not assigned to compound premium income across the same shares
Deploy capital systematically: ~$13k capital generated $68 premium this week (~0.52% weekly return)

strategy_candidates.change: After assignment loss risk, pre-set rules for when to sell calls at a loss (e.g., only sell calls above cost basis, or skip week if underwater >3%)
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth flagged that HIMS dropping to $47 (vs. $50 cost) creates assignment-at-loss risk if he sells $49 calls; he needs a rule to avoid this without missing premium weeks

title: Part 5.5 ... 7/4/25: $180 PER WEEK from HIMS stock?!
